content: All drone pilots, new or experienced, benefit from rules and safety tips to help you fly safe. More importantly, drone safety is the law. Find relevant resources below according to how you use your drone. Whether you re a public safety drone operator, certificated remote pilot, model aircraft flyer, or a drone racer, you re in the right place to learn about integrating these new entrants into our national airspace system. Getting Started Register Your Drone What Kind of Drone Flyer Are You? B4UFLY LAANC Temporary Flight Restrictions  TFRs  Remote ID Traveling with Your Drone Recreational Flyers The Recreational UAS Safety Test  TRUST  Where can I fly? Recreational Flyer Airspace Authorizations Certificated Remote Pilots including Commercial Operators Become a Drone Pilot Operations Over People Part 107 Waiver UAS Facility Maps Part 107 Airspace Authorizations Public Safety Operate a Drone, Start a Drone Program Understanding Your Authority  Handling Sightings and Reports Public Safety and Law Enforcement Toolkit FAA Contacts for Law Enforcement Emergency Situations Educational Users UAS Collegiate Training Initiative Youth Drone Initiative Advanced Operations Urban Air Mobility   Advanced Air Mobility Package Delivery by Drone Certification NEPA   Drones Dispensing Chemicals and Agricultural Products UAS Research, Development, Demonstration, and Testing UAS Test Site Program Broad Agency Announcement Program Connect with Us Contact Us Drone Events Programs, Partnerships   Opportunities Facebook   FAADroneZone Twitter   FAADroneZone Sign Up for UAS Email
